steps.title: Add equations to eliminate y
steps.content:
The two equations have $y$-coefficients of 4 and −4, making them ideal for elimination. Add them together:

$$(4y) + (−4y) = (9x + 14) + (5x − 23)$$

The $y$ terms cancel:

$$0 = 14x − 9$$

Rearrange:

$$14x = 9$$

steps.title: Calculate 56x by scaling
steps.content:
Notice that $56 = 14 \cdot 4$. Multiply both sides of $14x = 9$ by 4:

$$56x = 4 \cdot 9 = 36$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
